overview:
## Return Value

A result code. See [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/security-framework-result-codes].

## Discussion

If you use the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecTrustedApplicationCopyData(_:_:)] method to extract the data from a trusted app instance for storage or transmission, you can use the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecTrustedApplicationSetData(_:_:)] method to insert that data into a new trusted app. Doing so creates an object that identifies the same app as the original trusted app instance.

# SecTrustedApplicationSetData(_:_:)

**Function**

Sets the data of a given trusted app instance.

## Declaration

```swift
func SecTrustedApplicationSetData(_ appRef: SecTrustedApplication, _ data: CFData) -> OSStatus
```

## Parameters

- **appRef**: A trusted application object.
- **data**: A reference to the data to set in the trusted application.

## Return Value

A result code. See [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/security-framework-result-codes].

## Discussion

If you use the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecTrustedApplicationCopyData(_:_:)] method to extract the data from a trusted app instance for storage or transmission, you can use the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecTrustedApplicationSetData(_:_:)] method to insert that data into a new trusted app. Doing so creates an object that identifies the same app as the original trusted app instance.
title: SecTrustedApplicationSetData(_:_:)
